content: Diagnosis using Rotterdam criteria requires ≥2 of 3 features after excluding other causes: (1) Clinical/biochemical hyperandrogenism: hirsutism (modified Ferriman-Gallwey score ≥4-6 depending on ethnicity), severe acne, male-pattern alopecia; or elevated total/free testosterone, DHEA-S; (2) Ovulatory dysfunction: oligomenorrhea (cycles >35 days) or amenorrhea (>3 months without menses); (3) Polycystic ovaries on ultrasound: ≥20 follicles per ovary (2-9mm diameter) and/or ovarian volume >10mL. Exclusion criteria: thyroid dysfunction, hyperprolactinemia, non-classic congenital adrenal hyperplasia (17-OHP screening), Cushing syndrome, androgen-secreting tumors (rapid onset, virilization, very high testosterone). Workup: TSH, prolactin, 17-OHP, total/free testosterone, DHEA-S, pelvic ultrasound. Consider OGTT for glucose intolerance screening given high diabetes risk.
